Title: The chromosome-scale genome of Phoebe bournei reveals contrasting fates of terpene synthase (TPS)-a and TPS-b subfamilies

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Vegetative tissues, especially stems; stem bark highly enriched
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense against pathogens and herbivores; likely contributes to wood durability
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expanded and constitutively expressed TPS-a genes underlie high sesquiterpenoid accumulation in P. bournei
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: More retained copies; most TPS-a genes constitutively/highly expressed in root and stem tissues
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Tissue specificity; drought/cold profiling for family-wide expression divergence
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing PRJNA627308; RNA-seq PRJNA628065; VOC profiling by GC-MS
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The TPS-a subfamily expanded to 34 copies in P. bournei through tandem duplication. 2. More TPS-a copies were retained and constitutively expressed than TPS-b copies. 3. TPS-a genes are the principal contributors to abundant sesquiterpenoid accumulation and pathogen resistance in P. bournei.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome-wide family identification; phylogeny; tandem cluster analysis; RNA-seq expression profiling; GC-MS volatile profiling; transient functional assays of representative genes
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Subfamily-level core result central to paper; positive selection detected on several TPS-a branches
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Prioritize TPS-a retention/stacking to enhance sesquiterpene-based immunity in trees and crops

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Restricted/specific tissues, notably root xylem for some monoterpenes
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Although tandem-expanded, TPS-b genes experienced more loss/pseudogenization and lower/broader conditional expression, corresponding to low monoterpenoid accumulation in most tissues
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Many TPS-b genes underexpressed in analyzed tissues or expressed only under specific conditions/tissues
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Tissue specificity; drought/cold-responsive divergence at family level
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing PRJNA627308; RNA-seq PRJNA628065; VOC profiling by GC-MS
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The TPS-b subfamily also expanded by tandem duplication in P. bournei, reaching 28 copies. 2. Compared with TPS-a, TPS-b experienced higher gene loss and pseudogenization, with many fragments/pseudogenes. 3. The contrasting fate of TPS-b helps explain the relatively low monoterpenoid abundance in P. bournei.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome-wide family identification; phylogeny; tandem cluster reconstruction; RNA-seq expression profiling; VOC profiling
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Contains numerous fragments and pseudogenes, indicating contraction relative to TPS-a
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Restoring or introducing functional TPS-b genes could shift flux toward monoterpenes if desired

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter in transient overexpression assay
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase family; TPS N-terminal domain PF01397 and metal-binding C-terminal domain PF03936
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Farnesyl diphosphate (strongly inferable for sesquiterpene synthase)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes/rearranges farnesyl diphosphate to mainly β-caryophyllene and guaia-6,9-diene, with minor elemene and ylangene products
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Stem xylem and other vegetative tissues, especially stem
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antifungal defense; contributes to pathogen resistance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes β-caryophyllene accumulation; identified as one of the main synthases for β-caryophyllene in stem xylem
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: TPS-a genes are generally highly/constitutively expressed in root and/or stem tissues; PbTPS-a21 showed divergent expression from paralog PbTPS-a26
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High/constitutive expression in stem/root-associated tissues in P. bournei; product detected after transient expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Tissue context; drought and cold expression profiling performed for TPS family overall
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Gatekeeper scaffold-forming step for sesquiterpene diversity; not explicitly rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing BioProject PRJNA627308; RNA-seq BioProject PRJNA628065; metabolomics by GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PbTPS-a21 from chromosome 8 TPS-a clusters encoded a functional sesquiterpene synthase. 2. Transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana produced β-caryophyllene and guaia-6,9-diene as major products, plus elemene and ylangene as minor products. 3. PbTPS-a21 was identified as one of the main synthases underlying abundant β-caryophyllene accumulation in P. bournei stem xylem.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS product profiling; comparison with authentic standards; tissue VOC profiling; transcriptomics
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paralog of PbTPS-a26; interpreted as sub-/neofunctionalized relative to α-copaene-producing paralog
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering β-caryophyllene production and enhancing pathogen resistance in trees/crops; candidate for metabolic engineering of antifungal sesquiterpene traits

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter in transient overexpression assay
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes/rearranges farnesyl diphosphate to produce α-cubebene, α-copaene, and β-copaene
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Volatile defense-associated sesquiterpene contributing to scented wood
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes α-cubebene accumulation; identified as the main synthase for α-cubebene
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: TPS-a genes generally constitutively expressed in stem/root tissues
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected functionally in transient assay; tissue expression consistent with sesquiterpene-rich tissues
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Gatekeeper scaffold-forming step; not explicitly rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing BioProject PRJNA627308; RNA-seq BioProject PRJNA628065; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PbTPS-a22 is a functional TPS-a gene from chromosome 8. 2. Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana generated α-cubebene, α-copaene, and β-copaene. 3. PbTPS-a22 was identified as the main synthase for α-cubebene, one of the most abundant sesquiterpenes in P. bournei stem xylem.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS; comparison with tissue volatile profiles
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paralogous partner PbTPS-a23 did not show the same product accumulation profile, indicating functional divergence
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering woody aroma and defense-related sesquiterpene production

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter in transient overexpression assay
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Stem xylem and other vegetative tissues
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes β-caryophyllene accumulation; identified as one of the main β-caryophyllene synthases
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Divergent from paralogs; TPS-a genes overall highly expressed in roots/stems
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Product detected after transient expression in N. benthamiana; supports role in P. bournei stem sesquiterpene accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Gatekeeper scaffold-forming step; not explicitly rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing BioProject PRJNA627308; RNA-seq BioProject PRJNA628065; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PbTPS-a25 encoded a functional sesquiterpene synthase. 2. Overexpression of PbTPS-a25, but not its paralog PbTPS-a20, significantly enhanced β-caryophyllene emission in Nicotiana benthamiana. 3. PbTPS-a25 is a major synthase contributing to abundant β-caryophyllene in P. bournei stem xylem.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS; paralog comparison; tissue metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate to boost β-caryophyllene-mediated disease resistance in woody plants/crops

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter in transient overexpression assay
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes/rearranges farnesyl diphosphate to mainly tricyclic sesquiterpenes α-copaene and β-copaene
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Supported by transient assay in N. benthamiana; expression divergence noted in P. bournei tissues
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Gatekeeper scaffold-forming step; not explicitly rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing BioProject PRJNA627308; RNA-seq BioProject PRJNA628065; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PbTPS-a26 encoded a sesquiterpene synthase producing α-copaene and β-copaene. 2. It showed a distinct product profile from its paralog PbTPS-a21, indicating neofunctionalization/subfunctionalization. 3. The gene contributes to sesquiterpene diversification in P. bournei.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS; paralog functional comparison
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussion states PbTPS-a26 is the synthase for α-copaene, while paralog PbTPS-a21 synthesizes β-caryophyllene instead
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering sesquiterpene blend composition for defense/aroma traits
